architecture_summary: I-JEPA is a non-generative self-supervised method that uses a Vision Transformer context encoder, a target encoder updated by exponential moving average, and a narrow ViT predictor to predict target-block representations from a single context block in the same image. It relies on multi-block masking and computes the loss in representation space rather than pixel space.

novel_contributions: We introduce the Image-based Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (I-JEPA), a non-generative approach for self-supervised learning from images.
I-JEPA predicts representations of target blocks from a single context block in representation space rather than reconstructing pixels or tokens.
The paper proposes a multi-block masking strategy with sufficiently large target blocks and an informative spatially distributed context block.
I-JEPA is designed to learn semantic image representations without relying on hand-crafted data-augmentations.
The method is scalable and efficient, training a ViT-Huge/14 on ImageNet using 16 A100 GPUs in under 72 hours.

key_claims.claim: I-JEPA learns strong off-the-shelf representations without the use of hand-crafted view augmentations and outperforms pixel-reconstruction methods such as MAE on ImageNet-1K linear probing, semi-supervised 1% ImageNet-1K, and semantic transfer tasks.
I-JEPA is competitive with view-invariant pretraining approaches on semantic tasks and achieves better performance on low-level vision tasks such as object counting and depth prediction.

I-JEPA is scalable and efficient, with pretraining a ViT-H/14 on ImageNet requiring less than 1200 GPU hours and being over 2.5× faster than a ViT-S/16 pretrained with iBOT and over 10× more efficient than a ViT-H/14 pretrained with MAE.
The method uses a single context block to predict multiple target block representations in the same image, with the target encoder updated by exponential moving average.
Predicting in representation space is crucial: applying the loss in pixel space significantly degrades linear probing performance.
The proposed multi-block masking strategy is important for learning semantic representations, and larger target blocks with a sufficiently informative context improve low-shot accuracy.
I-JEPA achieves 87.1 top-1 accuracy when fine-tuned on the full ImageNet dataset, remaining within 1% of the best MAE model despite using far fewer pretraining epochs.
key_claims.evidence_section: D. Finetuning on the full ImageNet
